claimText: Namibia is the first African country to adopt the Unified Payments Interface (UPI) system.
evidenceExcerpt: Namibia is the first African country to adopt the UPI system for real-time digital payments.
examUse: Statement-based question on the internationalization of India's digital payment systems.
distractorHooks: South Africa or Kenya as the first African country
First European country instead of African

claimText: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is built over the Immediate Payment Service (IMPS) infrastructure.
evidenceExcerpt: UPI is built over the Immediate Payment Service (IMPS) infrastructure.
examUse: Statement-based question on the technical foundation and architecture of UPI.

claimText: NPCI has raised the per-UPI transaction limit to Rs 5 Lakh.
evidenceExcerpt: NPCI raised the per-UPI transaction limit to Rs 5 Lakh and the cumulative 24-hour limit to Rs 10 Lakh for Person to Merchant transactions.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multi-statement question on UPI transaction limits.
distractorHooks: Limit raised to Rs 10 Lakh per transaction
Limit raised to Rs 2 Lakh per transaction

claimText: NPCI International Payments Limited (NIPL) is a wholly owned subsidiary of NPCI incorporated to deploy RuPay and UPI outside India.
evidenceExcerpt: NIPL is a wholly owned subsidiary of NPCI incorporated in 2020 to deploy RuPay and UPI outside India.
examUse: Institution-feature question on NIPL's mandate and ownership.
distractorHooks: NIPL is a direct subsidiary of the RBI
NIPL manages domestic retail payments instead of international deployment

claimText: NPCI is a not-for-profit company initiated by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the Indian Banks' Association (IBA) under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
evidenceExcerpt: NPCI is a not-for-profit company initiated by RBI and IBA under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
examUse: Statement-based question on the establishment, nature, and statutory backing of NPCI.
distractorHooks: Initiated solely by the Ministry of Finance
Established under the RBI Act, 1934 or Companies Act without PSS Act backing
